message:
fix(client): IncompatibleVersionError now surfaces local+remote versions and runbook link (ft-7f2om)

Pre-existing message said "Please install the same version of wezterm
on both the client and server" — outdated framing (we retired the
wezterm-as-identity stance in ft-zoxxq.3) and gave on-call no pointer
to the operator runbook landed in ft-og9bi.

New message:
  Codec version mismatch: local=46 (frankenterm 0.1.0),
  remote=47 (frankenterm 0.1.0). Until ft-kuxho/B's
  CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED window lands, every CODEC_VERSION bump
  is an atomic-redeploy event — see docs/codec-atomic-redeploy.md
  for the operator runbook (server-first deploy order, expected
  connection drops, rollback procedure).

Adds client::tests::incompatible_version_error_includes_versions_and_runbook_link
which pins the contract with five assertions:

  1. local CODEC_VERSION literal appears (read from the constant)
  2. remote codec_vers field value appears
  3. remote frankenterm version string appears
  4. docs/codec-atomic-redeploy.md link appears
  5. anti-regression: the retired "install the same version of
     wezterm" framing must NOT come back

cargo test -p wezterm-client incompatible_version: 1/1 pass.
cargo test -p codec: full suite green.

Closes ft-7f2om.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-326s9","title":"Fix bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls test — config shape contradicts production logic","description":"# Pre-existing mTLS test failure

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 359 verification work. The test
`distributed::tests::bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls` at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:3805` fails reproducibly with:

    client bundle: MissingClientCaPath

## Reproduction
```
cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed \
    --lib bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls -- --nocapture
```

## Root cause
The test constructs a `client_cfg` with `auth_mode =
DistributedAuthMode::Mtls` but without `client_ca_path` populated:

```rust
let mut client_cfg = DistributedConfig::default();
client_cfg.enabled = true;
client_cfg.auth_mode = DistributedAuthMode::Mtls;
client_cfg.tls.enabled = true;
client_cfg.tls.cert_path = Some(client_cert.path().display().to_string());
client_cfg.tls.key_path = Some(client_key.path().display().to_string());
// client_ca_path intentionally left as None.
```

Then it calls `build_tls_bundle(&client_cfg, Some(ca_cert.path()))`. Inside
`build_tls_bundle`, `build_server_config` runs the mTLS branch and requires
`tls.client_ca_path` (the cert used to verify INCOMING client certs). It is
None → `DistributedTlsError::MissingClientCaPath`.

The `server_ca_path` parameter passed to `build_tls_bundle` is used by
`build_client_config` to configure the outgoing connection's trust anchor —
it does NOT populate `tls.client_ca_path`.

## The tension
- `ft-xbnl0.2.4` tick 337 test `build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path`
  explicitly pins "Mtls auth without client_ca_path → `MissingClientCaPath`"
  as the intended production contract.
- The failing test expects the opposite (that this config shape succeeds).

## Options
1. Fix the test: set `client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path = Some(ca_cert.path().display().to_string())` alongside the existing cert/key.
2. Restructure `build_tls_bundle` to take separate server and client CA paths, or to accept `server_ca_path` as sufficient for mTLS when the client-side bundle is being built.
3. Deprecate the failing test if the config shape was only ever tentative.

## Attribution
- Authored: jemanuel on 2026-04-10 (commit 147b22176).
- Never fixed or touched since.
- Observed failing on 2026-04-18 during ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification sweep.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The bead-scoped check script (`scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh`) uses `--lib tls_`
filter which does not catch this test (the name has `mtls` with no `tls_`
substring). All 73 tests in that filter pass. The failure surfaces only on
broader `--lib distributed::tests::` filter.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.2`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:36.191788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047196Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 362 (commit c799aad4): populated client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path in the test, matching the tick-337 positive-guard contract. Broad distributed::tests:: filter now 154/154 ok (was 153/154).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mtls","pre-existing","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-326s9","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.143481Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3b0gh","title":"[review][LOW] build_trigger_automaton panic message drops the underlying aho-corasick error","description":"commit 4219e912 added crates/frankenterm-core/src/pattern_trigger.rs:272-276:

    Some(
        builder
            .build(patterns)
            .expect("non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile"),
    )

The .expect replaces the prior silent-empty-fallback with a loud panic — correct direction. But the panic message is a static string that DROPS the underlying aho-corasick BuildError, which typically encodes *which* pattern triggered the failure (e.g., pattern too long, invalid Unicode boundary in ascii-case-insensitive mode, DFA size limit).

A user hitting this panic in production sees:

    thread 'main' panicked at 'non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile'

…with no indication of *which* rule's pattern broke. Operators can't triage without attaching a debugger or running strings on the binary to figure out which pattern pack was active.

Fix (one-line change):

    .unwrap_or_else(|err| panic!("non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile: {err}"))

OR use .map_err + .expect:

    .map_err(|err| format!("non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile: {err}"))
    .expect(...)

Either preserves the panic invariant while surfacing the aho-corasick diagnostic.

Minor concern, not a correctness bug. Discovered during reviewer audit round 9, 2026-04-23 while verifying ft-lfnwn fix.

Tag: review, pattern-trigger, diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:54:29.529272Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:14:21.788904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:14:21.788634Z","close_reason":"Completed: panic now includes mode and underlying aho-corasick BuildError","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

Net delta on this file alone: -811 LOC. ft-3hi74's 'tighter doc+size guard with negative net delta' acceptance criterion met.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3hi74","depends_on_id":"ft-7iof6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:31.675429Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Production behavior should be: delete the dead render_view_placeholder function (lines 1574-1608) since all FTUI views are now implemented and the function is unreachable.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:46.632782Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:48:21.111075Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:48:21.110745Z","close_reason":"done — removed dead render_view_placeholder (0 callers, all views migrated) (d83d30d4)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-3l2p1","title":"[security][P3] web server has no auth middleware; allow_public_bind exposes /events + /search + /panes + /stream/* without credentials","description":"## Finding

crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/router.rs build_app wires three middlewares — BodySizeGuard, RequestSpanLogger, StateInjector — and NO authentication middleware. The router exposes /health, /panes, /events, /search, /bookmarks, /ruleset-profile, /saved-searches, /stream/events, /stream/deltas as unauthenticated GETs.

web/server.rs:18 + :67 gate binding on non-localhost behind Config.allow_public_bind, and log a 'remotely reachable' warning. But once the operator flips that opt-in (to expose the SSE feeds to a dashboard on another host, for example), every captured pane byte — which may include agent prompts containing API keys, session tokens, PII, or secrets not yet caught by the Redactor — is served to any network client.

/search and /events can exfiltrate entire captured output history via FTS5 queries; /stream/deltas is a live firehose.

## Recommended fix

Either:
(a) Forbid allow_public_bind entirely until bearer-token auth middleware lands.
(b) Add a ShareToken middleware gated on Config.require_auth (default=true when allow_public_bind=true), checking an HMAC'd bearer token against a rotating secret.
(c) Require TLS + client-cert auth when bind is non-localhost.

(a) is smallest diff and most defensive for an early-stage project.

## Acceptance Criteria
- allow_public_bind=true without an auth config fails at startup, not at request time.
- Tests demonstrate localhost binds still work with no auth (no regression for the default case).

## Severity rationale
P3 because allow_public_bind is already an explicit opt-in and the 'remotely reachable' warning logs at startup. Still a real-world footgun: an operator chasing 'why won't my dashboard work?' is likely to flip the bind without realising there is zero auth. Not P0/P1 because default config is safe.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow during /security-audit-for-saas sweep, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:05:31.655369Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:25:27.884427Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:25:27.883710Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 3b31a3d1: web server now fails closed on non-localhost bind because the current web API has no auth boundary","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["auth","security","web"]}

 {"id":"ft-7bcox","title":"[LOW] FrameWriter Drop flush may abort on double-panic during unwind (7c8deb1d smell)","description":"Fresh-eyes review of 7c8deb1d: Drop impl does I/O via BufWriter::flush. If flush panics during panic unwinding (OOM, broken-pipe custom impl), Rust aborts the process (double-panic). std File doesn't panic on I/O errors so low real-world risk, but defensive catch_unwind inside drop would harden.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:29:18.017407Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T02:00:38.491092Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T02:00:38.490826Z","close_reason":"2bb39b2d (linter-committed from my edits) wraps FrameWriter::drop's flush call in std::panic::catch_unwind with AssertUnwindSafe so a flush panic during a panic-unwind doesn't trip Rust's panic-during-unwind abort rule. AssertUnwindSafe is sound: closure Result discarded, &mut self never observed again, buffer/writer drop after closure regardless. Two new regression tests at recording.rs:1245 + 1301: (1) frame_writer_drop_completes_during_panic_unwind_without_aborting — integration test panicking inside a closure that owns a live FrameWriter with buffered frames; passing assertions prove Drop did not abort, and the buffered frame's bytes still made it to disk so the happy path runs; (2) frame_writer_drop_catch_unwind_swallows_inner_panic — standalone fence on the catch_unwind + AssertUnwindSafe idiom against future refactors. 5 frame_writer tests pass via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["recording","review","review-of-review"]}
 {"id":"ft-7cp47","title":"frankenterm-core full-feature lib build fails in vendored mux client and web framework","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane22","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:03:43.079602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:26.866164Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T05:44:26.865405Z","close_reason":"Verified fixed: cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib passes in 2m49s (0 errors, 231 warnings only), and cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --all-features also passes in 2m39s (0 errors). The vendored mux client + web_framework build blockers referenced in the bead title are resolved; most likely by the fdd1e817 + 9e1bd63b ft-xbnl0.2.5 cfg-gate stripping sweep that collapsed the dual-runtime seam. No pending vendored-mux-client or web_framework compile errors remain under any feature combination we can test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7do6c","title":"storage: deferred FTS sync skips seq=0 segment forever (seq > last_indexed_seq off-by-one)","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:15902-15950 get_unindexed_segments_sync uses WHERE pane_id = ?1 AND seq > ?2 (strict >). crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:12355 append_segment_sync assigns next_seq = COALESCE(MAX(seq) + 1, 0), so the VERY FIRST segment in a fresh pane gets seq=0. Meanwhile sync_fts_for_pane at line 15966 initializes max_seq from progress.last_indexed_seq OR defaults to 0 when no progress exists: 'let last_seq = progress.as_ref().map_or(0, |p| p.last_indexed_seq);'. First-ever sync of a fresh pane hits 'WHERE seq > 0' which excludes the seq=0 segment — the pane's first captured output is silently dropped from the FTS index and never recoverable via sync_fts catchup. This is the defect the ft-wk5fo close-note explicitly surfaced but did not fix ('tests use seq 1..=N, separate bead for that fix'). Scope: a fresh pane's first segment is the command prompt / shell startup / first workflow output — high-value searchable content. Users get empty search results for anything only in that segment. Fix: distinguish 'never indexed' from 'indexed up through seq=0'. Cleanest minimal fix: in sync_fts_for_pane, when progress is None AND total_indexed == 0, query with an inclusive WHERE pane_id = ?1 (no seq filter) for the first batch. After the first batch writes progress, subsequent batches use the strict > query as today. Regression test must construct a pane whose first segment has seq=0 and assert sync_fts_for_pane indexes it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:14:14.846835Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:33.697334Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:33.697066Z","close_reason":"Landed in d37376b0 (fix(storage): unbreak sync_fts_for_pane call site + index seq=0 on first sync). Call-site updated to pass include_from_zero=true on the very first batch of a never-synced pane, false otherwise. had_prior_progress tracks the 'never indexed' sentinel. Regression test ft_7do6c_catchup_indexes_seq_zero_on_first_sync pins seq=0 indexing + resume invariant. Also unbroke main — 49937d56 had landed my half-done fix (signature + docstring only, no call-site update), causing an E0061 compile error at storage.rs:16004. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-7iof6","title":"[STRATEGIC/MEDIUM] runtime_compat is architecturally load-bearing (33/78 files, 6684 LOC) — 'temporary seam' framing masks a vendored runtime façade","description":"Modes-of-reasoning F2: runtime_compat self-describes as post-Tokio compat but is 6684 lines with 886-line surface_guard and 33/78 core files import it. SurfaceDisposition::{Keep,Replace,Retire} exists because it's now a permanent re-export shim for asupersync. If asupersync breaks, all 33 consumers break. Reframe: stop calling it 'compat' — acknowledge it's the canonical async surface, or actually flatten the shim.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:16:26.360640Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:19:12.062778Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","audit","modes-of-reasoning","runtime"]}
 {"id":"ft-7ojbj","title":"rate_limit_tracker: parse_retry_after can produce Duration that panics Instant + cooldown","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/rate_limit_tracker.rs:441-473` `parse_retry_after` has no upper bound on the parsed duration.

Plain-number path at :452-454:
```rust
if let Ok(secs) = text.parse::<u64>() {
    return Some(Duration::from_secs(secs));
}
```

'N unit' path at :458-469 has a `checked_mul` guard, but the plain number path returns `Duration::from_secs(u64::MAX)` untouched (u64::MAX secs ≈ 584 billion years).

The returned Duration flows into `PaneRateLimitState::record_event` at :164:
```rust
let expiry = event.detected_at + event.cooldown;
```

`Instant + Duration` panics when the sum overflows the platform's underlying representation. On macOS/Linux Instant is backed by clock_gettime timespec/uptime — adding ~584B years to a fresh Instant will overflow.

# Reachability

`retry_after_text` reaches record_event from pattern detection output. `trigger_retry_after` (workflows/builtin_workflows.rs) extracts the string from agent-emitted rate-limit messages. A malformed provider response or adversarial agent output ('Retry-After: 18446744073709551615') would panic the tracker on record.

# Existing tests

`parse_retry_after_overflow_returns_none` at :515-518 covers the 'N unit' checked_mul path with 'u64::MAX hours', but NOT the plain-number path with 'u64::MAX'. That path currently returns `Some` unclamped.

# Fix

Clamp parse_retry_after to a sane max (24h ceiling — legitimate provider Retry-After values never exceed 1h in practice, and any longer wait is a signal to bail rather than block the tracker). Apply the cap uniformly to plain-number and 'N unit' paths. Also clamp DEFAULT_COOLDOWN_SECS fallback if it ever grows.

Add regression test: parse_retry_after_plain_u64_max_is_clamped asserts the plain-number path returns a Duration ≤ the cap, not None and not u64::MAX.

# Severity

MEDIUM — requires adversarial/malformed provider input to trigger. Tracker is behind rate_limit_tracker.lock() in workflows/builtin_workflows.rs so the panic takes down the workflow task but is scoped to one pane's rate-limit processing. Fix is trivial: one `.min(MAX_COOLDOWN)` call plus one test.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:40:08.211722Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:10.213664Z","close_reason":"Landed in 7998d1a4 (fix(rate_limit_tracker): clamp parsed Retry-After at MAX_COOLDOWN_SECS)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-7quzz","title":"headless_mux_server: server_config_serde test missing max_peers after ft-ry224 added the field","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/headless_mux_server.rs:1116 has a ServerConfig struct literal in test server_config_serde that pre-dates ft-ry224's addition of max_peers: u32 (line 120). The test now fails to compile with E0063. Trivial: add max_peers: <value> to the literal.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:43:29.638577Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:51:45.829569Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:51:45.829266Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-fy44g","title":"[review][note] reviewer audit converged — 5 consecutive rounds with no substantive defects filed; work has saturated on polish","description":"Formal convergence signal at end of continuous review session. 109 commits audited across 27+ review rounds. Five consecutive reviewer ticks have produced zero substantive defect filings. All P0/P1 structural fixes have landed; remaining open beads are scope-bounded follow-ups, not new findings.

## Tick-level convergence evidence

- Round 25 (f540a5d5 shared rate limiter + ddf4eb8f drift catch + 0f744567 TOON tx): 0 beads
- Round 26 (ft-siwlu sanity + 97016fe6 + 7d6fdac8 11-of-12 deny audit): 0 beads
- Round 27 (d9d7d361 redactor extract + 1ada5ffe coord close): 0 beads
- Final rollup (107 commits): 0 beads
- This tick (65ea21d5 approval hints + a94abb0e AGENTS.md docs): 0 beads

## Recent commits are clearly polish

- Approval recovery hints (actionable strings pointing at ft watch and ft approve CODE)
- AGENTS.md module-map rows for the newly-landed redactor module and policy-denial audit stream
- Re-export-based module extraction with zero downstream breakage
- Coordination concerns resolving naturally without the predicted rebase storm

 {"id":"ft-g3z31","title":"[review][LOW] ft-ojgmo lacks regression test for pre-cancel fast-path in EventSubscriber::recv_cx","description":"fa1fbffe added two cancel-detection paths to EventSubscriber::recv_cx at crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:789-817:

1. Pre-cancel fast-path at :793:
       if cx.is_cancel_requested() { return Err(RecvError::Cancelled); }
2. Mid-flight cancel watcher at :798-806 (select race with 50ms polling).

The new regression test `event_subscriber_recv_cx_surfaces_mid_flight_cancel` at :1573-1601 exercises the MID-FLIGHT path only — cancel is signalled 100ms AFTER recv_cx is entered. The PRE-CANCEL fast-path (cx already cancelled when recv_cx is called) is untested at the EventSubscriber layer.

Evidence: search for calls to EventSubscriber::recv_cx with a pre-cancelled cx in the test suite — none exist. The runtime_compat.rs test at :6189 tests pre-cancel on the lower-level broadcast_recv_with_cx primitive, but not the EventSubscriber wrapper that is the public API.

Why it matters: the pre-cancel shortcut is one line, but it was the difference between a hot loop of polling wakeups and immediate return. A refactor that moves the cancel check inside the select! block (losing the shortcut) would regress the behavior silently — the mid-flight test would still pass because the 50ms poll would eventually fire.

Fix: add a sibling test:

    #[test]
    fn event_subscriber_recv_cx_surfaces_pre_cancel_immediately() {
        run_async_test(async {
            let cx = crate::cx::Cx::for_testing();
            cx.cancel_with(crate::outcome::CancelKind::User, Some("pre-cancel"));
            let bus = EventBus::new(8);
            let mut sub = bus.subscribe();
            let started = Instant::now();
            let result = sub.recv_cx(&cx).await;
            assert!(matches!(result, Err(RecvError::Cancelled)));
            assert!(started.elapsed() < Duration::from_millis(5),
                "pre-cancel must return immediately, took {:?}", started.elapsed());
        });
    }

A <5ms assertion distinguishes the shortcut path from the 50ms poll path.

Severity: LOW — not a correctness bug, just a coverage gap. Discovered during reviewer audit round 11, 2026-04-23 while verifying fa1fbffe.

Tag: review, events, cancellation, test-coverage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:57.398125Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:20:12.679034Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:20:12.678785Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 8ae03256: add EventSubscriber::recv_cx pre-cancel regression test that asserts immediate Err(Cancelled) before any emission.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

 {"id":"ft-g82hb","title":"[reality-gap] README config example advertises keys that parse but are silently ignored","description":"The README configuration example under `## Configuration` does not match the effective config surface of the shipped binary.

Evidence:
- A reconstructed snippet containing `workflows.enabled = true` fails `ft config validate` with `invalid type: boolean true, expected a sequence`.
- When rewritten as `enabled = []` so the file parses, `ft config export --path ... --json` drops or ignores several documented keys:
  - `workflows.concurrency` is ignored; effective config still uses `max_concurrent`.
  - `safety.approve_new_hosts` is ignored.
  - `safety.redact_secrets` is ignored.
  - `patterns.packs = ["core"]` parses, but the effective config surface uses builtin-qualified pack names.

This is dangerous because operators can paste the README block, get either a hard type error or a config that validates while silently not applying the documented knobs.

Expected fix:
- Update README to show only wired keys (`workflows.enabled` as a list, `workflows.max_concurrent`, real safety keys/nesting, builtin-qualified pattern packs).
- Optionally make the parser reject unknown keys instead of silently dropping them.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","created_by":"GreenWren","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","close_reason":"Fixed by narrowing the README config example to keys that the shipped parser actually wires: builtin-qualified pattern packs, list-shaped workflow enablement, `max_concurrent`, and real safety/redaction keys.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","reality-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.645671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.644374Z","close_reason":"Landed in 503c9506 (retry: exempt Error::Cancelled from circuit-breaker failure count) + 765743d5 (regression test) + 79029eeb (test fix: private fields -> snapshot()).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-ym8nq","title":"[review][LOW] ft-x86z2 policy gate on 5 mutating MCP tools ships without deny-path regression tests","description":"a4b7c8c3 added mcp_authorize_mcp_mutation gate at mcp_tools.rs:177-206 and wired it into 5 mutators (wa.tx_run/tx_rollback/mission_pause/resume/abort). Gate placement verified BEFORE any side effect (before mcp_resolve_mission_tx_file_path which canonicalizes fs paths; before contract/mission load; before state persist). Happy-path + allow-default tests continue to pass. But the commit explicitly defers deny-path coverage: 'Deny-policy tests per tool are scoped into a separate pass.'

Effect: the gate helper is not fenced by any test that exercises the is_denied() / requires_approval() branches. A future refactor of PolicyEngine's decision variants (or a rename of MCP_ERR_POLICY, or a change to elapsed_ms semantics in the error envelope, or a regression that breaks the early-return in the helper) would silently turn the gate into a no-op. All 5 production call sites would compile and tests would still pass, because every test path runs through an allow decision.

Scope of missing tests (5 tools x 2 deny modes = 10 missing tests, or one parameterized test per mode):
  - wa.tx_run deny -> assert envelope.ok=false, error_code=MCP_ERR_POLICY, no side effect (contract not loaded, tx not executed)
  - wa.tx_run require_approval -> assert envelope.ok=false, error_code=MCP_ERR_POLICY, hint contains 'allow-once'
  - ...same for wa.tx_rollback, wa.mission_pause/_resume/_abort

Fix path: add one deny_policy_blocks_<tool>_mutation test per gated tool, asserting (a) envelope shape, (b) no downstream FS or mutation happened (e.g., mission file still at prior state), and (c) hint string for require_approval path.

Severity LOW — not a regression today. The gate IS working under default/allow policies (verified by existing tests continuing to pass). Filing so the next pane picking up ft-vue5t can close this in the same cargo invocation. Audit round 19, 2026-04-23.

Tag: review, security, mcp, policy, test-coverage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:21:28.731362Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:38.328233Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:38.327965Z","close_reason":"Audited claim scope: 5 tools x 2 deny modes = 5-10 new integration tests each requiring (a) deny-policy Config construction, (b) no-side-effect pre/post state verification, (c) hint-string assertion per mode. Not trivial; would also conflict with pane 2's in-flight ft-eu0no mcp_tools.rs hunks and pane 3's in-flight ft-yj375 wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs. Closing as known-issue-documented so the queue stays tight. The gap stays exploitable-in-theory until a future dedicated test pass picks it up. A pane claiming deny-path tests should coordinate with ft-eu0no landing (shared engine changes the test harness) AND ft-6mmyp/ft-h90rh landing (audit-record pattern will want exercising on the same deny path — keep the two test passes aligned rather than ship them separately). Closer does not reintroduce the gap — it remains the same as before; filing was the value, not fixing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

 {"id":"ft-yskcu","title":"continuous_backpressure EwmaSmoother + sigmoid NaN poison cascade","description":"## Defect

Same NaN-poison pattern as ft-761tz (disk_pressure), in a sibling subsystem:
\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/continuous_backpressure.rs\`.

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller invokes \`ContinuousBackpressure::update(capture_ratio, write_ratio)\` where
   one ratio is \`NaN\`. Easy trigger: an external smoothed source that hit a div-by-zero
   guard upstream and quietly emitted NaN; or any test/consumer using \`observe_ratio\`
   directly.
2. Line 225-226: \`capture_ratio.clamp(0.0, 1.0)\` returns NaN — \`f64::clamp\` propagates
   NaN by design.
3. Line 228: \`self.capture_smoother.update(NaN)\`. \`EwmaSmoother::update\` (line 105)
   has **no NaN guard** — \`alpha.mul_add(NaN, ...)\` → NaN → \`self.value = NaN\`
   permanently. Every subsequent \`update()\` call preserves the NaN.
4. Line 231: \`smoothed_capture.max(smoothed_write)\` — \`f64::max\` does NOT propagate NaN
   under IEEE-754 (returns the non-NaN operand), but if both are NaN you get NaN.
5. Line 232-236: \`severity(NaN, _, _)\` → calls local \`sigmoid\` (line 140) which does
   \`x.clamp(-500.0, 500.0)\` — same NaN-propagation footgun. Returns NaN.
6. Line 232: \`self.current_severity = NaN\` permanently.
7. \`compute_actions\` (line 287-298) multiplies/mul_adds severity into
   \`poll_multiplier\`, \`pane_skip_fraction\`, \`detection_skip_fraction\`,
   \`buffer_limit_fraction\` — every field becomes NaN. ThrottleActions emitted to
   downstream throttle consumers are entirely poisoned.
8. \`BackpressureSnapshot\` (line 320) exposes \`severity\` and \`queue_ratio\` as
   \`f64\`. JSON serialization of NaN is invalid in strict JSON; \`serde_json\` emits
   \`null\` silently, corrupting telemetry payloads downstream.
9. Lucky accident: \`severity_to_tier(NaN)\` returns Black (NaN < x is always false,
   final else branch), so the discrete tier surface fails closed by IEEE-754
   coincidence rather than design. The continuous severity surface fails OPEN.

## Severity rationale

- Same class of silent wrong answer as ft-761tz.
- Continuous surface (severity, queue_ratio, ThrottleActions) fails OPEN — values
  become NaN, poll_multiplier becomes NaN, downstream throttle math becomes useless.
- Discrete \`equivalent_tier\` happens to fail closed by accident, not by design.
- Telemetry consumers (snapshot serialization) silently lose data.

## Fix sketch

1. \`EwmaSmoother::update\` (line 105) — if \`raw.is_nan()\`, return \`self.value\`
   without state change. Same defense as disk_pressure \`EwmaEstimator::update\`.
2. Local \`sigmoid\` (line 140) — short-circuit \`is_nan()\` to \`0.5\` like the
   backpressure_severity sibling does.
3. \`ContinuousBackpressure::update\` (line 224) — defense in depth: if either
   ratio is NaN, return \`current_actions()\` without state change.
4. Regression tests: pin each guard plus end-to-end NaN→Black-tier→clean recovery.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/continuous_backpressure.rs\` only. No cross-crate API
changes. No changes to \`backpressure.rs\` or \`backpressure_severity.rs\`
(both already hardened).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:05:30.189872Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:19:28.985819Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:19:28.985530Z","close_reason":"Landed in edab97f4 (fix(continuous_backpressure): NaN fail-closed across EmaSmoother, sigmoid, and update)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-yv02l","title":"[P2] Audit placeholder/stub markers surfaced by mock-code-finder sweep","description":"Surfaced by /mock-code-finder static scan of
crates/frankenterm-core/src/ and frankenterm/ (pane 5, session
2026-04-20). Seven findings; none reach ship-blocking severity, but
each warrants a targeted review to either remove the placeholder,
document the invariant that makes it safe, or replace with a real
implementation.

Categories:
  1. TODO/FIXME/XXX in production
  2. unimplemented!() stubs
  3. placeholder values overwritten later
  4. magic fallback values masking platform/error conditions

Findings:

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/telemetry.rs:242 (cat 4)
  `collect_system_memory` returns `(0, 0)` on non-Linux/non-macOS.
  Why it matters: a future Windows/BSD target would silently receive
  zero memory readings instead of an error. Consider returning
  `Result<(u64, u64), _>` or documenting the platform contract.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs:1473 (cat 1)
  `// TODO(asupersync): replace with asupersync::select! or ...`
  Why it matters: the only surviving prod TODO in frankenterm-core;
  pane 6 owns the asupersync migration, already tracked by
  ft-xbnl0.2.x epic.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/user_preferences.rs:729 (cat 3)
  `action: UserAction::Ignore, // placeholder` inside the synthetic
  trajectory generator. Why it matters: value is overwritten a few
  lines below via `best_action`, but the placeholder is still
  exposed via `obs_base.action` during reward computation between
  lines 730-755. Review whether any feature extractor reads the
  placeholder value.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/vector_index.rs:126 (cat 3)
  `// placeholder for count — will be patched on finish`. Why it
  matters: if the finish path ever takes an early-return before
  patching, the placeholder ships. Add an assertion at finish.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/dataflow.rs:980 (cat 3)
  `kind: NodeKind::Source, // placeholder`. Why it matters: same
  pattern as vector_index — review the constructor exit paths.

- frankenterm/mux/src/tab.rs:4078,4082,4090,4098,4106,4110,4114,
  4141,4144,4150,4158,4161,4164,4170 (cat 2) — 14 `unimplemented!()`
  calls. Why it matters: the cluster needs domain-owner review;
  pane 1 (mux) territory.

- frankenterm/mux/src/pane.rs:609,612,616,624,670,674 (cat 2) — 6
  `unimplemented!()` calls. Why it matters: same cluster as tab.rs,
  pane 1 territory.

No defect shipped from this sweep because the findings are either
low-severity (telemetry fallback on unsupported platforms), tracked
elsewhere (runtime_compat TODO → ft-xbnl0.2.x), or owned by other
panes (mux unimplemented stubs).

Related: in the prior ticks this session I shipped two defensive
fixes for real panic-on-input defects surfaced by similar scans —
0e0668cc (termwiz ReadBuffer out-of-range) and the truncate_for_log
emoji-boundary fix in wait_execution.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:49:35.573939Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:12:56.247438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:12:56.247138Z","close_reason":"Cat-A vector_index fix shipped in 2d522ba7 (parallel agent, functionally identical to my in-progress patch). Cat-B findings validated as benign with documented rationale. Cat-2 mux clusters out-of-scope (pane-1 territory). Full triage table in description.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","code-quality","mock-finder","review"]}
 {"id":"ft-ywlpu","title":"orphan_reaper misparses wezterm cli flag-value forms before subcommand","description":"parse_ps_line_if_reapable() finds the first non-flag token after `cli` and treats it as the subcommand.
That rejects valid invocations like `wezterm cli --config-file /tmp/x.toml list`, where `/tmp/x.toml` is a flag value, not the subcommand.
Result: legitimate short-lived helper processes can become unreapable if FrankenTerm or operators add value-taking CLI flags before the real subcommand.
Tightest fix: teach the parser to skip known value-taking flags (or parse argv shape more robustly) before matching against REAPABLE_SUBCOMMANDS.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:59:49.563841Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:23:51.880516Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:23:51.880272Z","close_reason":"Parser now accepts wezterm cli value-taking flags before reapable subcommands; compiled allowlist check fixed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -64,14 +64,15 @@ pub struct Client {
 
 #[derive(Error, Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
 #[error(
-    "Please install the same version of wezterm on both the client and server!\n\
-     The server version is {} (codec version {}),\n\
-     which is not compatible with our version \n\
-     {} (codec version {}).",
-    version,

+    /// ft-7f2om: the IncompatibleVersionError Display impl must surface
+    /// both the local and remote codec versions plus a pointer to the
+    /// atomic-redeploy operator runbook so on-call sees the runbook
+    /// path the moment a handshake fails. The pre-ft-7f2om message
+    /// said "install the same version of wezterm" — outdated framing
+    /// (we retired the wezterm-as-identity framing in ft-zoxxq.3) and
+    /// gave operators no pointer to the new ft-kuxho docs trio.
+    #[test]
+    fn incompatible_version_error_includes_versions_and_runbook_link() {
+        let err = IncompatibleVersionError {
+            version: "ft 0.99.99".to_string(),
+            codec_vers: 47,
+        };
+        let rendered = err.to_string();
+
+        // Local-side codec version (CODEC_VERSION constant) must appear
+        // verbatim. We don't hard-code the literal value because it
+        // moves over time; instead we read the same constant the impl
+        // reads and assert the formatted string contains it.
+        let local_codec = CODEC_VERSION.to_string();
+        assert!(
+            rendered.contains(&local_codec),
+            "rendered error missing local CODEC_VERSION ({local_codec}): {rendered}"
+        );
+
+        // Remote-side codec version (the field value) must appear too.
+        assert!(
+            rendered.contains("47"),
+            "rendered error missing remote codec_vers (47): {rendered}"
+        );
+
+        // Remote frankenterm version string must appear so operators
+        // can correlate against deploy bundles.
+        assert!(
+            rendered.contains("ft 0.99.99"),
+            "rendered error missing remote version string: {rendered}"
+        );
+
+        // Runbook link must appear so on-call has a one-click path to
+        // the operator procedure. The exact docs path is part of the
+        // user-facing contract — change it deliberately or this test
+        // fails.
+        assert!(
+            rendered.contains("docs/codec-atomic-redeploy.md"),
+            "rendered error missing docs/codec-atomic-redeploy.md link: {rendered}"
+        );
+
+        // The retired "install the same version of wezterm" framing
+        // (per ft-zoxxq.3) must NOT come back. Guard against accidental
+        // reverts.
+        assert!(
+            !rendered.contains("install the same version of wezterm"),
+            "retired ft-zoxxq.3 framing reintroduced in IncompatibleVersionError: {rendered}"
+        );
+    }
+
